sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 2.16 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.2)

statement.latex: A group$G$is called conjugacy separable\/ if any two of its elements are conjugate in$G$if and only if their images are conjugate in every finite homomorphic image of~$G$. Is$G$conjugacy separable in the following cases:$G$is a polycyclic group,$G$is a free soluble group,$G$is a group of (all) integral matrices,$G$is a finitely generated group of matrices,$G$is a finitely generated metabelian grou...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
